summary: Addressed the domain_designer REQUEST_CHANGES by clarifying risk-tier semantics in README and adding deterministic decision-aid wording to riskPenalty score explanations while preserving the exact documented scoring formula.

tests_run.result: PASS: focused calculateOpportunityScore tests passed, including high-risk and exclusion-level risk explanation assertions.
PASS: Next.js production build completed successfully. Nonfatal local SWC native-package warnings were printed before fallback build continued.
PASS: no whitespace errors. Git printed line-ending conversion warnings for README.md and project state JSON files.

decisions: Preserved the exact weighted formula and did not add hidden filtering, ranking side effects, persistence, API, UI, database, provider, network, or orchestration work.

Expanded README risk-tier semantics to state: 0-10 low risk can proceed, 11-35 medium risk needs caution/disclaimer/checklist framing, 36-70 high risk is poor automation/default-ranking fit, and 71-100 should be filtered or treated as a do-not-build signal outside the numeric total.
Added deterministic riskDecisionAid() wording for medium, high, and exclusion-level penalties so downstream scoreExplanation carries product decision guidance.
Added focused assertions that high-risk explanations mention poor automation fit and review before ranking, and exclusion-level explanations mention do-not-build handling outside the numeric total.
risks: Risk penalty still has the documented 0.06 subtractive weight, so later scan orchestration or ranking policy should filter exclusion-level opportunities explicitly rather than relying on totalScore alone.
Risk explanation wording is deterministic MVP guidance and may need richer evidence-aware text after real scan data is available.
The successful build still emitted nonfatal local Next SWC native-package warnings about next-swc.win32-x64-msvc being an invalid Win32 application.
requested_action: Re-review the risk-tier semantic clarification requested in the domain_designer handoff and route to qa_reviewer if accepted.
